name: charset-normalizer (Character Encoding Detection)

performance.notes: Pure Python with optional C extension; faster than chardet

agent_readiness.notes: Detect and decode unknown character encodings. from charset_normalizer import from_bytes, from_path, from_fp. result = from_bytes(raw_bytes). result.best(). str(result.best()). result.best().encoding. result.best().encoding_aliases. from_path('file.txt'). CLI: python -m charset_normalizer --help. Replaces chardet in requests library (since 2.26). normalize('file.txt', 'output.txt'). Supports 40+ encodings. Coherence score for confidence.

gotchas: Probabilistic detection — short texts have lower accuracy
ASCII and UTF-8 are always correctly detected — uncertainty only in legacy encodings
Best() can return None for ambiguous input
Replaced chardet in requests 2.26 — can swap in most use cases
best_when: Detecting encoding of files from legacy systems (Windows-1252, ISO-8859-x)
Web scraping where Content-Type header encoding is wrong or missing
Replacing chardet with more accurate and faster alternative
avoid_when: UTF-8 encoded files (can detect directly without heuristics)
When chardet is already integrated and working
summary: charset-normalizer detects and decodes character encodings. from_bytes(raw). result.best().encoding. from_path('file.txt'). 40+ encodings. Replaced chardet in requests 2.26. MIT by Ahmed Tahri. More accurate and faster than chardet for most cases. Short texts have lower detection accuracy. Returns None from best() on ambiguous input. CLI: python -m charset_normalizer.
